subject: [PATCH 1/2] btrfs: don't finalize fs roots during qgroup snapshot accounting

reviews.author: Sun YangKai
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Sun YangKai pointed out that the patch is generally well-received but requested minor implementation details to be addressed, specifically suggesting improvements to the code's organization and naming conventions.

Reviewer Sun YangKai suggested using an unsigned int for the 'ret' variable, as it's only set by radix_tree_gang_lookup_tag which returns an unsigned int, and also questioned the name 'ret' since it's never returned.

Reviewer Sun YangKai suggested modifying the index calculation in qgroup_commit_fs_roots() to prevent potential array out-of-bounds access, proposing a change from `index = ret;` to `index=btrfs_root_id(gang[ret-1]) + 1;`.

Reviewer suggested optimizing the code by updating the 'index' variable outside the loop, which is not used inside the loop, to improve readability.
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you for your work on this \u2014 much appreciated.

I am not very familiar with this part of the codebase, so please take my 
comments with that in mind.

That said, I have a few minor inline suggestions regarding some of the 
implementation details. Nothing major \u2014 just small things I noticed 
while reading through. Hope they help. Please see below.

On 2026/4/24 06:43, Leo Martins wrote:

---

I wonder if it would be better to use unsigned int for ret since it only 
set by radix_tree_gang_lookup_tag which returns unsigned int.

And I wonder if ret is a good name since we never return ret.

---

I assume `ret > ARRAAY_SIZE(gang)` should never happen here so maybe we 
could have `index=btrfs_root_id(gang[ret-1]) + 1;` here.

---

index seems not used inside this for loop, so maybe we can update it 
outside the loop to make it looks better.

Thanks again for the patch.
Regards,
Sun YangKai

2026-04-24.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Btrfs file system to avoid finalizing file system roots during qgroup snapshot accounting, which can lead to incorrect state and silent corruption of snapshots. The patch introduces a new helper function `prepare_root_item` that updates root items without the side effects of finalization, and a new function `qgroup_commit_fs_roots` that iterates over dirty roots using an advancing index instead of clearing tags. This change prevents premature clearing of `BTRFS_ROOT_FORCE_COW`, freeing of log trees, and other finalization actions during transaction progress, ensuring consistent qgroup accounting and preventing potential corruption issues.

2026-04-23.patch_summary: This patch modifies the Btrfs file system to avoid finalizing file system roots during qgroup snapshot accounting, which can lead to incorrect state and silent corruption of snapshots. The patch introduces a new helper function `prepare_root_item` that updates root items without the side effects of finalization, and a new function `qgroup_commit_fs_roots` that iterates over dirty roots using an advancing index instead of clearing tags. This change prevents premature clearing of `BTRFS_ROOT_FORCE_COW`, freeing of log trees, and other finalization actions during transaction progress, ensuring consistent qgroup accounting and preventing potential corruption issues.
